metrics.context_qualifier: Rabies vaccinations provided to street dogs and pets in Dharamsala during the reporting year.
Sterilization procedures performed on street dogs in Dharamsala to control population growth.
Emergency veterinary care and rehabilitation provided to street animals in critical condition.
Expansion of the Brake for Strays street mural program led to a reduction in road accidents and animal deaths.

counterfactual_baseline.description: 17% reduction in hit and run cases and 13.5% less deaths caused by road accidents compared to previous period.

metrics.evidence_quote: This represents a total 6 lives saved, and 10 less amputations.
metrics.metric_name: Lives Saved by Road Safety Murals

metrics.context_qualifier: Expansion of the Brake for Strays street mural program led to a reduction in severe injuries requiring amputation.

counterfactual_baseline.description: 17% reduction in hit and run cases and 13.5% less deaths caused by road accidents compared to previous period.

metrics.evidence_quote: This represents a total 6 lives saved, and 10 less amputations.
metrics.metric_name: Amputations Prevented by Road Safety Murals

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs found forever homes through local and international adoption programs.

significant_events.event_name: Strategic Partnership with Humane World for Animals
significant_events.source_quote: 2025 also brought a new strategic partnership with Humane World for Animals (formerly Humane Society of the USA/International). DAR was chosen to be part of their Veterinary Training and Capacity Building Program.
significant_events.summary: DAR partnered with Humane World for Animals for a Veterinary Training and Capacity Building Program, including training for vets, catchers, and community outreach.

significant_events.source_quote: 1,860 vaccinations 11 days. A 13% increase over 2024. This Camp was funded by Mission Rabies and Vets Beyond Borders Denmark.
significant_events.summary: An annual rabies vaccination and awareness camp provided 1,860 vaccinations over 11 days, a 13% increase over 2024, funded by Mission Rabies and Vets Beyond Borders Denmark.

significant_events.source_quote: In July, we added Himani Negi to our DAR Team as Community Engagement Manager. This staffing investment helped us expand and connect with more than 636 adults and children in person and reach thousands more via radio shows and press coverage.
significant_events.summary: Himani Negi was hired as Community Engagement Manager to expand outreach, connecting with over 636 adults and children in person and thousands via radio and press.

significant_events.event_name: Brake for Strays Street Mural Program Expansion
significant_events.source_quote: Thanks to your contributions, we expanded our Brake for Strays street mural program 2025, adding two new murals near busy and deadly intersections.
significant_events.summary: The Brake for Strays street mural program was expanded with two new murals, totaling 5, leading to a 17% reduction in hit and run cases.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2025, your targeted donations helped us replace mission-critical equipment like our cauterizer, laptops, and our ambulance. You also helped us upgrade our diagnostic capabilities with a biochemistry analyzer.
significant_events.summary: Targeted donations helped replace mission-critical equipment like a cauterizer, laptops, and an ambulance, and added a biochemistry analyzer to upgrade diagnostic capabilities.

significant_events.event_name: Featured in 'Tails of the Himalayas...of Dogs and Devotees' Documentary
significant_events.source_quote: DAR is Featured in Tails of the Himalayas...of Dogs and Devotees Documentary, scheduled to be released this year.
significant_events.summary: DAR was featured in a documentary, 'Tails of the Himalayas...of Dogs & Devotees', scheduled for 2026 release, highlighting their work with India's strays.

data.original_text: Not only do they provide care for the large stray dog population (an estimated 5000 in Dharamsala) but also to local pets and farm animals.
We visit schools and villages to educate them about rabies and compassion towards animals.
Target demographics: to end the human/street dog conflict. Direct beneficiaries per year: last year we helped 3951 animals. Geographic areas served: Dharamsala India.
Dharamsala Animal Rescue is constantly working to end human death caused by rabies through humane animal control, rescue work, adoption and education.
DAR has implemented a humane and effective program for creating a healthy sustained population of stray dogs which directly impacts community health.
We treat animals in critical condition at our clinic and recovery center. Car accidents, wire traps, illnesses, skin diseases, and maggot wounds are some of the issues we see.
Since that time, we have helped more than 18,500 animals.
Results: We have helped over 23,000 animals.
Founded in 2008, Dharamsala Animal Rescue has been helping 1500 animals annually through their programs and community outreach.
With only 9 full time staff members Dharamsala Animal Rescue is able to spay/neuter roughly 500 animals each year.
